metric_value: BAAN IV classified as 'Dynamic-ERP'; competitors classified as 'Static-ERP'
notes: Aberdeen coined Dynamic vs Static ERP framework in this study

metric_value: Three key criteria: process-oriented, best-practices-based, supports continuous business improvement
notes: Aberdeen research-derived criteria framing Baan as uniquely qualified

metric_value: Orgware is an important decision factor for companies choosing Baan solutions
notes: Aberdeen customer research corroborating Orgware's sales value

metric_value: Up to 450 customer-specific production-ready business objects, functions and workflows
notes: Vendor-cited; Aberdeen did not independently benchmark

metric_value: Four components: Business Function Model, Business Process Model, Business Organization Model, BAAN IV Configurator
notes: Architectural decomposition directly from Baan product documentation

metric_name: Dynamic ERP requirement: changing business model
metric_value: True Dynamic-ERP must encompass a continually changing business model (new, reconfigured or time-phased)
notes: Factor 1 of 3 in Aberdeen's Dynamic-ERP definitional framework

metric_name: Dynamic ERP requirement: re-generate business objects
metric_value: Must completely re-generate and link underlying business objects encompassing application logic flow
notes: Factor 2 of 3 in Aberdeen's Dynamic-ERP definitional framework

metric_value: Must generate customized executable system without manually setting system parameters, auth codes or user interfaces
notes: Factor 3 of 3 in Aberdeen's Dynamic-ERP definitional framework

metric_value: Static ERP customers invest in upfront modeling that does not carry through to implemented system due to serial, disconnected process
notes: Aberdeen's critique of SAP and peer systems implied but not named

metric_value: Aberdeen believes Baan enjoys at least a 12-month advantage over major ERP competitors
notes: Key forward-looking claim; rivals include SAP, Oracle, PeopleSoft

metric_value: Baan's lead evaporated by 1998: revenue manipulation scandal, share price dropped 95% from peak, customers (Siemens, Carrier) switched to SAP
notes: Source: Wikipedia/Baan Corporation, Accountancy Age 2000; Aberdeen's 12-month lead prediction proved incorrect

metric_value: Aberdeen recommends Baan be on every serious ERP evaluation shortlist
notes: Strong Aberdeen endorsement driving consideration among enterprise buyers

metric_value: Baan sold to Invensys for $700M in 2000 (peak market cap was ~$7B); then sold to SSA Global for $135M in 2003; absorbed into Infor LN by 2006
notes: Source: CNET/Invensys, Wikipedia; Baan failed as independent entity

metric_value: Technology leadership + marketing agility + strategic wins (Boeing) to displace incumbent ERP vendors
notes: Aberdeen frames Baan's rise from 'nowhere' as driven by product and deal-making

metric_name: Business process modeling as key ERP differentiator
metric_value: Aberdeen concludes that inherently dynamic BPM capability is 'the key value-added technology' for next-century manufacturing ERP
notes: Prescient in that BPM and model-driven config became mainstream ERP requirements

metric_value: Aberdeen asserts Static-ERP must give way to Dynamic-ERP with internet enablement capabilities
notes: Internet-enabled ERP prediction proved correct as a trend, but Baan was not the winner

metric_value: Two model types: Reference Model (industry best practices) and Project Model (company-specific)
notes: Architectural detail from product announcement

metric_value: Three failure modes: elongated config/implementation times, rigidly imposed predefined practices, plethora of configuration parameters
notes: Aberdeen's characterization of incumbent ERP vendors' weaknesses

metric_value: Boeing selected Baan over SAP and Oracle in 1994 in landmark competitive win for Baan
notes: Source: Wikipedia/Baan; mentioned in study as context for Baan's credibility

metric_value: Covers manufacturing, finance, distribution, transportation, services, and project management
notes: Full functional scope of BAAN IV as described in the study
